channel.title: Statistics on Eric Windsor
channel.description: Recent content in Statistics on Eric Windsor

item.title: Distribution
item.description: Probability distribution PDF(probability density function) Definition : $f$ is a continuous function with: $f(x) \ge 0, \forall x$ $\int_{-x}^{x} dx = 1$ $P(a<x<b) = \int_a^bdx$ Random variables - usually denoted by uppercase letters near the end of our alphabet （e.g. X, Y）. Particular value - now use lowercase letters, such as x, which correspond to the r.v. X. if $f$ is a continuous function and $P(x=a)=P(a\le x \le a)=\int_a^af(x)dx=0,a\in\mathbb{R}$ $$ P(x<=a) \equiv P(x

item.title: Possibility
item.description: Intro Possibility: calculating the likelihood of future events Statistics: gathering, and analyzing data Machine learning: models which are accurate most of the time. It contains 2 important factors, accuracy (how close the predictions are from the true value) and robustness (the ability to generalize to unknown data) Possibility Sample space ($\Omega$) is a set of all possible outcomes or results of a random experiment. Points $\omega$ in $\Omega$ are called sample outcomes, realizations, or elements.
